data.content: This is an **Android tombstone** crash log from a .NET MAUI app. Let me analyze it and attempt to download symbols for the Mono runtime frames.

arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5; dpkg -l | grep -i binutils 2>/dev/null | head -5

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try to download libmonosgen-2.0.so symbols from Microsoft symbol server
# BuildId: 1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"

# Try the standard ELF symbol server URL patterns
echo "=== Trying Microsoft Symbol Server ==="
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code} - %{url_effective}\n" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" 2>&1

curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code} - %{url_effective}\n" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" 2>&1

# NuGet symbol server
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code} - %{url_effective}\n" \
  "https://symbols.nuget.org/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" 2>&1

curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code} - %{url_effective}\n" \
  "https://symbols.nuget.org/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" 2>&1
arguments.description: Download symbols from Microsoft symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Download symbols from Microsoft symbol server

data.reasoningText:
This is an Android tombstone/crash log (not an Apple .ips crash log), so the apple-crash-symbolication skill is not appropriate here. Let me analyze this crash log manually.

The crash is from a .NET MAUI app (`com.companyname.MauiTest`) running on an Android arm64 device. The key details:

- **Signal**: SIGABRT (signal 6)
- **Abort message**: 'not compatible'
- **Library**: `libmonosgen-2.0.so` — this is the Mono runtime used by .NET MAUI on Android
- **BuildId**: `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`


- **Crash location**: The runtime frames (#01-#07) are all in `libmonosgen-2.0.so`, with frame #08 pointing to JIT-compiled managed code in an anonymous mapping

To symbolicate this, I'd need the debug symbols for `libmonosgen-2.0.so` matching that specific BuildId, then use tools like `addr2line` or `llvm-symbolizer` to resolve the addresses to function names and source locations.

Let me try fetching the symbols from Microsoft's symbol server using the BuildId to construct the download URL.

arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; dpkg -l | grep -i llvm 2>/dev/null | head -5; dpkg -l | grep -i binutils 2>/dev/null | head -5

# Try to download libmonosgen-2.0.so symbols from Microsoft symbol server
# BuildId: 1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"

# Try the standard ELF symbol server URL patterns
echo "=== Trying Microsoft Symbol Server ==="
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code} - %{url_effective}\n" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" 2>&1

curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code} - %{url_effective}\n" \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" 2>&1

# NuGet symbol server
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code} - %{url_effective}\n" \
  "https://symbols.nuget.org/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" 2>&1

curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code} - %{url_effective}\n" \
  "https://symbols.nuget.org/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" 2>&1
arguments.description: Download symbols from Microsoft symbol server

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-86961400a7644199a9d9783671f61fd2

# Use aarch64 addr2line since the binary is ARM64
ADDRS="0x222098 0x2241ac 0x1abc80 0x1abd14 0x16dfa4 0x16db6c 0x16de04"

echo "=== Symbolicating .NET runtime frames (libmonosgen-2.0.so) ==="
echo ""
FRAME=1
for addr in $ADDRS; do
  result=$(aarch64-linux-gnu-addr2line -C -f -e _.debug "$addr" 2>&1)
  func=$(echo "$result" | head -1)
  loc=$(echo "$result" | tail -1)
  printf "  #%02d  pc %-18s  %s\n                              %s\n\n" "$FRAME" "$addr" "$func" "$loc"
  FRAME=$((FRAME + 1))
done

data.reasoningText:
We have the debug symbols file. It's an ELF 64-bit ARM aarch64 file with debug info. Now let me use `addr2line` (specifically the aarch64 cross version since this is an ARM64 binary and we're on x86_64) to symbolicate the addresses.

The addresses from the crash log for libmonosgen-2.0.so frames:
- #01: 0x222098
- #02: 0x2241ac
- #03: 0x1abc80
- #04: 0x1abd14
- #05: 0x16dfa4


- #06: 0x16db6c
- #07: 0x16de04

Now I'll run addr2line with the aarch64 cross-compiled binutils to resolve these addresses to their source locations.
